name: fin_journal_lines
title: Journal Lines
description: Debit/credit rows belonging to one journal. A line is a debit OR a credit -- nothing in this schema enforces that split (a row could carry both debit_cents and credit_cents nonzero), matching the source's own JournalLine model, which also never enforced it at the field level (reconciled against a private predecessor-system audit, not part of this repo: 'JournalLine (debit-or-credit, ...)' is a naming/usage convention, not a guarded constraint). Totals are never stamped back onto fin_journals -- see object_finance.journal_totals for the computed fold and packages/app-finance/schemas/fin_journals.json's status field help for why no balance check exists at post.

fields.name: journal_id
fields.label: Journal
fields.help: Set once at creation and not intended to change after. Not schema read_only -- same reason as app-invoices' invoice_lines.invoice_id: a required, client-submitted-at-creation field cannot also be read_only on this platform (the server rejects any client submission of a read_only field, even the first one).

fields.name: entity_id
fields.label: Entity
fields.help: The set of books this belongs to (65 multi-entity). Optional during rollout; backfilled to the owner's default entity, set by the entity switcher on new writes.
